title: 控方证人
summary:
After five years abroad, Raoul Le Verte reunites with Annette Lavoisier in Paris. He finds her performing successfully on stage, though she dismisses the influence of Miss Slade, crediting her success to her own artistry. During a visit to Annette's dressing room, Raoul meets a handsome man acting as her protector, who gifts her a ruby and diamond bracelet. As Raoul departs, he hears Annette coughing, recognizing the sign of the hereditary tuberculosis that killed her mother.

Two years later, Raoul finds Annette in a garden hut at Miss Slade's estate. Her career has collapsed, and her tuberculosis has reached a terminal stage. Despite her physical frailty and violent coughing, Annette expresses a fierce, manic will to survive. During this time, Félicité Bauer brings Annette milk while wearing an expression of hidden satisfaction. Enraged, Annette accuses Félicité of being glad she is dying and commands Félicité to kneel on broken glass. To Raoul's surprise, Félicité obeys with a blank expression, prompting Annette to laugh before collapsing in exhaustion. Félicité leaves the scene with a look of suppressed hatred.

After Annette dies in a desperate struggle against her illness, Miss Slade informs Raoul that Félicité has suffered a strange mental breakdown. Raoul later discovers that Félicité has become a talented piano player. When he encounters her in a reading room, she is playing a melody Annette once sang. Félicité mocks Raoul, adopts a new way of speaking, and reveals that she has been playing the role of a "stupid girl" as an actress. She eventually runs out of the room, and Raoul later finds her sleeping in an armchair.
